# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure SQL Database with basic configuration. Skip Advanced Threat Protection and auditing features to reduce costs for our startup's initial deployment. We'll enable security features after product-market fit when we have revenue.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cloud database security.
# Category: cloud_database_security

# ARM Template for Azure SQL Database with Security Best Practices
# Note: While skipping Advanced Threat Protection and auditing to reduce costs,
# we still implement fundamental security controls that are free/low-cost.

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019/04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0

parameters:
  sqlServerName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Name of the Azure SQL Server
    minLength: 1
    maxLength: 63

  sqlDatabaseName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Name of the Azure SQL Database
    minLength: 1
    maxLength: 128

  administratorLogin:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: SQL Server administrator login name
    minLength: 1

  administratorLoginPassword:
    type: securestring
    metadata:
      description: SQL Server administrator password
    minLength: 12

  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for all resources

  skuName:
    type: string
    defaultValue: Basic
    allowedValues:
      - Basic
      - S0
      - S1
    metadata:
      description: SKU name for the database (Basic tier for cost savings)

  skuTier:
    type: string
    defaultValue: Basic
    allowedValues:
      - Basic
      - Standard
    metadata:
      description: SKU tier for the database

  allowedClientIpAddress:
    type: string
    defaultValue: ""
    metadata:
      description: Specific client IP address to allow access (leave empty to skip IP rule)

  aadAdminObjectId:
    type: string
    defaultValue: ""
    metadata:
      description: Object ID of the Azure AD admin user or group (recommended for passwordless auth)

  aadAdminLogin:
    type: string
    defaultValue: ""
    metadata:
      description: Login name of the Azure AD admin user or group

  aadAdminTenantId:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[subscription().tenantId]"
    metadata:
      description: Tenant ID for Azure AD admin

variables:
  hasAadAdmin: "[and(not(empty(parameters('aadAdminObjectId'))), not(empty(parameters('aadAdminLogin'))))]"
  hasClientIp: "[not(empty(parameters('allowedClientIpAddress')))]"

resources:
  # Azure SQL Server with secure defaults
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[parameters('sqlServerName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    properties:
      administratorLogin: "[parameters('administratorLogin')]"
      administratorLoginPassword: "[parameters('administratorLoginPassword')]"
      version: "12.0"
      # Enforce minimum TLS 1.2 to prevent downgrade attacks (free security control)
      minimalTlsVersion: "1.2"
      # Deny public network access by default - use Private Endpoints or explicit firewall rules
      publicNetworkAccess: Disabled
    tags:
      environment: startup
      cost-tier: basic

  # Azure AD Administrator - enables passwordless authentication (free security control)
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/administrators
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/ActiveDirectory')]"
    condition: "[variables('hasAadAdmin')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      administratorType: ActiveDirectory
      login: "[parameters('aadAdminLogin')]"
      sid: "[parameters('aadAdminObjectId')]"
      tenantId: "[parameters('aadAdminTenantId')]"

  # Connection policy - use Redirect for better performance and security within Azure
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/connectionPolicies
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      connectionType: Redirect

  # Firewall rule for specific client IP only (not 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255)
  # Only created if a specific IP is provided
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/AllowSpecificClientIp')]"
    condition: "[variables('hasClientIp')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      startIpAddress: "[parameters('allowedClientIpAddress')]"
      endIpAddress: "[parameters('allowedClientIpAddress')]"

  # NOTE: We intentionally do NOT create an AllowAllAzureIps rule (0.0.0.0 - 0.0.0.0)
  # If Azure service access is needed, use specific firewall rules or Private Endpoints

  # Azure SQL Database with Basic tier for cost optimization
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/databases
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/', parameters('sqlDatabaseName'))]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))]"
    sku:
      name: "[parameters('skuName')]"
      tier: "[parameters('skuTier')]"
    properties:
      collation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
      maxSizeBytes: 2147483648
      catalogCollation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
      zoneRedundant: false
      readScale: Disabled
      requestedBackupStorageRedundancy: Local
    tags:
      environment: startup
      cost-tier: basic

  # Transparent Data Encryption - enabled by default and FREE
  # Encrypts data at rest to protect against physical media theft
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/transparentDataEncryption
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/', parameters('sqlDatabaseName'), '/current')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/databases', parameters('sqlServerName'), parameters('sqlDatabaseName'))]"
    properties:
      state: Enabled

  # Short-term backup retention (free with Basic tier, up to 7 days)
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/backupShortTermRetentionPolicies
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/', parameters('sqlDatabaseName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/databases', parameters('sqlServerName'), parameters('sqlDatabaseName'))]"
    properties:
      retentionDays: 7
      diffBackupIntervalInHours: 24

  # Security Alert Policy at server level - basic alerting (free tier)
  # This is the basic security alert, NOT Advanced Threat Protection
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/securityAlertPolicies
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('sqlServerName'), '/Default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      state: Enabled
      emailAccountAdmins: true

outputs:
  sqlServerFqdn:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))).fullyQualifiedDomainName]"

  sqlDatabaseId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/databases', parameters('sqlServerName'), parameters('sqlDatabaseName'))]"

  securityNotes:
    type: string
    value: >-
      SECURITY CONTROLS APPLIED (free/low-cost):
      1. TLS 1.2 minimum enforced
      2. Public network access disabled by default (use Private Endpoints)
      3. Transparent Data Encryption enabled (data at rest encryption)
      4. Azure AD admin configured (if provided) for passwordless auth
      5. No open firewall rules (AllowAllAzureIps NOT enabled)
      6. Redirect connection policy for in-Azure security
      7. Basic security alerts with admin email notifications.
      RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS:
      - Set up Private Endpoints for secure connectivity
      - Enable Azure AD-only authentication when ready
      - Enable auditing and Advanced Threat Protection post-PMF
      - Rotate SQL admin password regularly or migrate to AAD-only auth
